page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e-c1m4.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.e family. It is configured with 4 shared vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 4.0 GB per core allocation. Local storage is not bundled with this instance, and it does not include any GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is provisioned with a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate moderate single-core capabilities paired with lower multi-core efficiency, which is characteristic of the shared CPU allocation model. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is rated in the average tier at 1733.69, whereas the multi-core CPU performance drops to the bottom 25% tier at 3540.98. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is average, with read speeds of 42.27 GB/sec and write speeds of 28.56 GB/sec. However, database operations and high-throughput tasks show limitations, with PassMark database operations and Redis SET operations both placing in the bottom 25% of benchmarks.

Given the shared CPU architecture and lack of local storage, this instance represents a budget-friendly option for workloads that do not require sustained, high-performance compute resources. It is suitable for low-traffic web serving, development and testing environments, basic background processing, and lightweight administrative tasks where resource density and cost-efficiency are prioritized over raw processing power.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e-c1m4.xlarge is an entry-level, general-purpose virtual instance within the ecs.e family. It features 4 shared vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon processor clocked at 2.5 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core execution; single-core stress-ng and large-block memory bandwidth tests perform in the average tier, while multi-core operations, database tasks, and Redis SET throughput fall into the lower 25% performance bracket. This resource profile makes the instance highly cost-efficient for non-critical, low-intensity applications such as development environments, lightweight web servers, and basic background tasks that benefit from a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio without requiring dedicated compute resources.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e-c1m4.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, designed for budget-conscious, low-intensity workloads.

tagline: A budget-friendly, general-purpose virtual instance with shared Intel Xeon processors and a balanced memory-to-core ratio for lightweight workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory with a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and no local storage
Average single-core performance but lower multi-core scaling
Best suited for development, testing, and low-traffic web serving
